page: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes a substantial 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple multi-threaded and memory-bound workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, ranking in the top 10% for both small-block cached and large-block uncached read and write operations. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, alongside static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the average middle 50% tier.

The D128ds_v6 offers high resource density, combining a massive vCPU count with a large local storage capacity. This profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized enterprise workloads that can leverage the 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The server is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a dedicated x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, offering 64 physical cores, 128 vCPUs, and 512.0 GB of RAM. The server includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, but lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving, while single-core performance remains in the average tier. This high-density configuration is well-suited for parallelized enterprise workloads, large-scale database management, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ds_v6 is a 128-vCPU virtual machine with 512 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 128-vCPU platform with massive local storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
512 GB of system memory paired with 7559 GB of local storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
Excellent fit for large-scale database operations and high-throughput web serving
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX
